findings.title: ETH-strike premium payouts revert because WETH unwrapping is blocked by `receive()`

best_agent_match.title: ETH-settled premiums can be permanently DOSed because WETH unwrapping is rejected

findings.title: Unvalidated `acoToken` metadata can be abused to sweep arbitrary ERC20 balances held by the writer

best_agent_match.title: Arbitrary `acoToken` metadata lets an attacker sweep any ERC20 balance held by the writer

findings.title: Premium and ETH payouts use global contract balances, letting the next caller drain leftovers

best_agent_match.title: Global-balance payouts allow the next caller to steal prior ETH or premium balances

findings.title: Newly minted options are sent to the caller, but the sale logic only sells the writer's old balance

best_agent_match.title: Newly minted options are sent to the caller, while the sale logic only sells the contract's old balance

findings.title: Using `transfer` for final ETH payout lets contract callers DOS writes that return ETH

best_agent_match.title: Using `transfer` for final ETH payout makes contract wallets and integrators easy to DOS

rejected_candidates.title: Reentrancy vulnerability in _sellACOTokens function
rejected_candidates.reason: `write()` is protected by `nonReentrant` for the entire execution, `_sellACOTokens` is only reachable from `write()`, and there is no alternate state-changing entry point to exploit via reentry.

rejected_candidates.title: Missing SafeMath library causing arithmetic overflow/underflow
rejected_candidates.reason: The contract performs no meaningful arithmetic beyond comparisons and balance passthroughs, so there is no concrete overflow/underflow attack surface here.

rejected_candidates.title: Incorrect ETH value validation - msg.value not checked against collateralAmount
rejected_candidates.reason: While the UX is poor, the code does not silently retain excess ETH; remaining ETH is forwarded to the user-selected exchange and then any leftover balance is returned to the caller.

rejected_candidates.title: No validation of acoToken address - can call arbitrary addresses
rejected_candidates.reason: Merged into the more specific reportable finding about fake `acoToken` metadata being used to sweep arbitrary ERC20 balances from the writer.

rejected_candidates.title: No refund of excess ETH when using ERC20 collateral
rejected_candidates.reason: Incorrect: if ETH remains after the exchange call, the contract explicitly sends `address(this).balance` back to `msg.sender`.

rejected_candidates.title: Missing return value check on balance check
rejected_candidates.reason: This is only malformed-token compatibility risk; `abi.decode` reverts on bad return data, so it does not create a realistic protocol-level exploit.
